observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected line items of Operating and Investing Activities in the Statement of Cash Flows. Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash flows from operating, investing and financing activities are presented in a manner which is most appropriate to its activities which would provide information that allows users to assess the impact of those activities on the financial position of the LGU and the amount of its cash and cash equivalents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the subsidiary ledger and entries affecting the Discount on Real Property Tax subsequently preparing the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books and to ensure adherence to correct accounting entries in recording of discount on advance payments in the ensuing years for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing department (e.g. Engineering Office) to investigate and reconcile those projects that were already completed and close the same to the appropriate PPE account.
Require the Engineering Office to provide the Accounting Office with copies of a comprehensive report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and
Based on the adjusted data, require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to explain the treatment of the OPAPP funds and to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the accounts affected by the transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to finalize a report to determine the propriety and reliability of accounts and if proven to be no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and attach required documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the stale checks and prepare an adjusting entry to ensure the correct and proper presentation of the account the Cash In Bank and Accounts Payable balances in the financial position.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to correctly record in the books of SEF the respective expenses account and submit the lacking documentary requirements, including certification from DepEd that none of the expenditures were charged from the DepEd’s budget and allocation, to justify the validity and propriety of the claims for reimbursements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to review the accounts, identify/trace the unreconciled variance, and prepare the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books upon verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the Loan Payables – Domestic between Current and Non-Current Liabilities in the financial statements;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile, prepare, and submit a Reconciliation of Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis and Actual Amounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the reconciliation and submit the reports required in COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the required Physical Inventory Plan to be reviewed and approved by the LCE and submitted to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities for the ensuing years; and
Conduct the physical count efficiently and without delay following the PIP, and submit the required reports such as the RPCPPE, Inventory Count Form, List of PPEs found at the Station, and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs within the designated period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the SDO to submit the Liquidation Reports for all cash advances for the Confidential Fund to the COA-ICFAU;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that additional cash advances for CF and IF are granted only upon his/her certification, stamped, or printed on the DV that previous cash advances have been liquidated and proper accounting thereof has been made; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to explain why the cash advances were granted despite the absence of supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for biological assets, update the same based on the report of the Municipal Agriculturist and ensure that proper accounting and recognition is made to the Financial Statements as well as to the Notes to the Financial Statements;
Instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to determine the value of the remaining livestock not recorded in the LGU's books and to coordinate with the GSO when receiving livestock or items from other agencies to ensure comprehensive recording of all items and properties in the future; and
Require the Municipal Agriculturist to prepare and submit a necroscopy report for every death of livestock owned by the LGU and submit the same to COA for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the audit team with all the loan agreements/contract entered in to by the municipality for their official review and evaluations; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the amortization schedules of Loan Payables-Domestic and append the same schedule in the disbursement vouchers of all future loan payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all Collecting Officers to diligently turn over all collections to the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer daily for those assigned at the Office and at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00 for field collectors to prevent any unnecessary delays.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Designate a duly authorized and bonded accountable officer at the Office of Municipal Civil Registrar, if warranted; and
Require a definitive determination or designation regarding the accountability of the Head of MEEDO as a collecting officer, otherwise immediately cease the performance of collection-related functions to ensure compliance with the established rules and regulations of handling collections and accountable forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the timely submission of the Consolidated RAAF, and likewise instruct all Collecting Officers to submit their respective monthly RAAF within, in strict compliance with the applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all active accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance with the Public Bonding Law and strictly comply with the fidelity bond requirement by applying for additional bond for those accountable officers with insufficient amount of bonds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the GSO to avoid using the Petty Cash Fund to pay for regular and recurring expenses, particularly Fuel, Oil and Lubricant Expenses; and
Require the GSO to revisit the monthly budget for petty operating expenses of the office and adjust the amount of the PCF granted to the petty cash custodian if necessary to comply with Section 48 of the MNGAS for LGUs and Section 4.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to refrain from hiring unnecessary consultancy services which pertain particularly to the roles and functions that are within the level of expertise of the in-house capacity of the regular employees of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare a report of lacking DVs, cumulative and updated, every month and ensure that relevant information such as check number, JEV number, amount, payee, and nature of the transaction is provided in the report whenever incomplete submission of monthly DVs cannot be avoided; and
Explain the non-submission of the subject DVs;
Submit the 60 unsubmitted DVs for January to October and those DVs for the month of November and December not later than May 30, 2025 to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the BAC Secretariat to submit all approved contracts together with its supporting documents and to ensure that all prospective contracts are submitted within 5 days upon its perfection; and
Revisit and identify the gaps in the BAC and, if warranted, constitute a new member/committee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the MPDO to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the Municipal POPS Fund; and
Direct the LCE, MBO and MPDC to seek the approval of the Sanggunian before the implementation of a specific project in the event that projects in the approved budget were expressed in general terms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MPDO to submit a copy of the POPS plan indicating the abovementioned projects and duly approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the Provincial DILG;
Ensure that utilization of the POPS Fund must be for programs and activities that aims to promote and attain a safe, secure, and peaceful community in line with the concerted POPS-Plan 2023-2025 of the DILG and in compliance with DILG issuances relating to POPS; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging expenditures against the POPS Fund for PPAs which does not correspond with those outlined under DILG MC No. 2022-118 dated September 19, 2022 and MC No. 2019-143 dated August 27, 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to inform COA within 10 days after the infrastructure project is awarded or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices have already been posted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) stop charging administrative expenses against the LDRRMF to provide sufficient funds for the LDRRM programs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MDRRMO to fast-track the implementation of PPAs; and
Require the MDRRMO to ensure that PPAs of LDRRMF are fully implemented at the end of the year in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Local School Board to submit the approved contract of the procured Janitorial Services charged to the SEF for CY 2024, including all its integral parts, and all the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the JEVs, DVs and all the supporting documents required under COA Circular 2012-001 to substantiate the claims against the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as co-chairman of the Local School Board, to explain the reason for hiring the LGU focal person to DepEd and provide for the duties and responsibilities performed to avoid any possible disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the reconciliation and submit the reports required in the COA Circular; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the required Physical Inventory Plan to be reviewed and approved by the LCE and submitted to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities for the ensuing years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer, and Municipal Engineer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of construction materials inventory; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards to track the actual Materials Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the GSO to record, monitor, and account for all properties strictly,
acquired through donations and to provide a copy of the deed of donation or other supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office to serve as the basis for the recording;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the subject PPEs and inventories received through donations; and
Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to establish strong internal control, formulate policies, and adopt close coordination in the receipt and issuances of inventories and properties of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Review and analyze each fund/ program or project, specifically dormant and negative balances under the account, and undertake the necessary action to cleanse and bring the account to its correct balance;
Inquire in writing from the Source Agencies of the idle/dormant accounts, regarding the proper disposition of the balance of fund transfers, if warranted; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the accounts of RHU to the Trust Liabilities account, the Provincial Shares to Due to LGU account, and to use the Due to NGAS instead of the Due to Other NGAS account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT collected in 2023 and to strictly observe the correct accounting entries in recording advance payments in the ensuing years for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the MEEDO and Municipal Treasurer to submit a report providing details on the collections and uncollected rentals from the lessee to the Municipal Accountant to serve as a basis in the recording of transactions;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entries; and
Direct the MEEDO to collect the unpaid stall rentals from the lessee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the collections in the trust liability account instead of the miscellaneous income account;
Submit copies of the Sangguniang Resolution/s, Deed of Turnover and Acceptance, and individual contracts of the beneficiaries/buyers of the housing units; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to monitor the status of the project and submit a record of the courses of action adopted by his Office in view of the delay incurred by the contractor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare a complete disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements using the required format;
Ensure that the Report on Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization is correct and supported with verifiable documents and supply the information on the Continuing Appropriations and Previous Year's Appropriations Transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and
Maintain a separate registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for LDRRMF QRF, MOOE and CO and to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year of transfer for the unutilized DRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to stop issuing checks without sufficient funds;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer’s Office to adopt appropriate measures to control the cash availability as well to safeguard its assets and check the accuracy and reliability of its data; and
Explain in writing why the MT should not be administratively charged for drawing checks with insufficient funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of the encashment of checks by utilizing the daily collections and to deposit intact and daily all collections pursuant to the regulations mentioned above;
Require all Collecting Officers to diligently turn over all collections to the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer daily for those assigned at the Office and at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00 for field collectors to prevent any unnecessary delays; and
Require all Collecting Officers to prepare the RCD at the close of each business day.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the Accountable Forms issued to the AOs by releasing stubs one at a time and to review and reconcile the RAAF of each AO to ensure the correctness of the CRAAF prior to its submission to the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Comply strictly with the fidelity bond requirement by applying for additional bonds for those accountable officers with insufficient amounts of bonds; and
Ensure that the GSO and all active accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance with the Public Bonding Law.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer the immediate release of the shares due to the different LGUs; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timely release of RPT shares in order that the funds can be timely used to finance the various programs and projects of the LGUs concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the GSO to explain the late payment of insurance; to ensure that all motor vehicles, heavy equipment, and machinery of the LGU have insurance coverage with GSIS and to pay the annual insurance regularly; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to be meticulous when using the insurance expense account and to consider the period of insurance coverage.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the history repair of motor vehicles is attached to the disbursement vouchers; and
Require the GSO to properly accomplish the waste material report and pre and post-inspection reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Statement of Work Accomplished every month for every infrastructure project implemented by administration; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that DVs are supported with complete and proper documents as enumerated in the different COA Circulars.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. 	Ensure that utilization of the POPS Fund must be for programs and activities that aim to promote and attain a safe, secure, and peaceful community in line with the concerted POPS-Plan 2023-2025 of the DILG and in compliance with DILG issuances relating to POPS; and
b. 	Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging expenditures against the POPS Fund for PPAs which does not correspond with those outlined under DILG MC No. 2022-118 dated September 19, 2022, and MC No. 2019-143 dated August 27, 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the GF for the 20% EDF, with complete SLs using the Sub-codes for this Special Account; and to prepare a post-closing trial Balance and Financial Statements for this Special Account and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification in the ensuing year; and
Require the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Treasurer upon authorization from Sanggunian Bayan, to open a separate bank account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) stop charging administrative expenses to LDRRMF to provide sufficient funds for the LDRRM programs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a report of lacking DVs, cumulative and updated, every month and ensure that relevant information such as check number, JEV number, amount, payee, and nature of the transaction is provided in the report whenever incomplete submission of monthly DVs cannot be avoided; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to explain the non-submission of the subject DVs and to submit the 195 DVs not later than April 5, 2024 to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the 2023 trial balance, ensure the timely submission of the financial reports in the ensuing periods, and revisit the workload distribution among the Accounting personnel to ensure timely submission of reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB and Commission on Audit (COA) at the end of each semester and to observe the same in the years thereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to assign personnel who will consolidate and prepare the report, to devise a timeline for the submission of response from each department/office concerned with the audit recommendations, and to ensure adherence to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the BAC Committee to explain the mode of procurement adopted in the payment of food packs;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking relief distribution list, i.d. or vaccination card and DV 101-21-11-1650 with check number 78945662 dated Nov. 19, 2021; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that checks are prepared under the official name of the business entity.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the GSO to strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit the CY 2021 Inventory Report to the Audit Team;
Direct the Inventory Committee to invite any representative from the Commission on Audit to witness the conduct of the actual physical inventory taking; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to update the Property Ledger Cards by using the appropriate form.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of consolidating the various payables under the name of “No Payee”;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to devise a schedule and cut off period of recording obligations of the Municipality at year end and to ensure that all officials and employees specially Department Heads are informed in advance of the schedule; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of these unidentified payees together with its supporting documents and to ensure that future transactions involving the recognition of account payables are always supported with purchase order, delivery receipts and other pertinent documents to support the claim.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit a written explanation supported with a (i) detailed schedule on all payments made on the loan and (ii) provide a copy of the computation of the penalty, otherwise, the Accountable Officer is required to refund the said amount pursuant to Section 105 of PD 1445 ; and to strictly monitor the payment schedule of all loans and ensure that better controls are installed in protecting government funds against loss or wastage.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are
always supported with appropriate and complete documents as enumerated in Section 5.14 of COA Circular 2012-001;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute the monetization of leave credits pursuant to DBM Budget Circular 2016-02 for future transactions; and
Request the Municipal Budget Officer to check the availability of funds for the release of the underpayment of monetization.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that prospective payments for advance payment are duly accompanied by a guarantee or bond in the form and amount specified under Section 4, Annex E, of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct all Department Heads, BAC Secretariat and GSO to be meticulous and judicious in the preparation of their PPMP and Annual Procurement Plan so that procurements made by the municipality are properly planned and executed, and procurements made through alternative methods do not result to splitting of government contracts.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Supply Office and Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that goods are described as to their type/kind and other relevant specifications in all transaction documents.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers not later than March 15, 2022; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit at the beginning of every year the required list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year; to inform COA within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and to submit monthly updates and quarterly reports on government projects.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF to trust fund books;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile and to prepare the necessary corrections in the different reports; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances for continuing appropriation of the LDRRMF.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to undertake extensive review of the current balances of the liabilities to ensure that previous contributions were all remitted; to ensure that the exact amount of future premiums/loan amortizations are withheld and promptly remitted to these government-owned and controlled corporations; and to ensure that no advance payment of contributions will be made.
We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to direct the Collecting Officers to remit/deposit intact daily all collections to the treasury of the agency to protect the funds of the government.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to:
Restrict access to cash accountable forms and their records to authorized personnel only to reduce the risk of unauthorized use and/or loss to the government.
Strictly implement the procedures on the requisitions of accountable forms and daily turnover of collections to strengthen the Municipality’s control over collections.
See to it that all collectors are properly supervised and their accountabilities are regularly monitored.
Require the erring accountable officer to remit the collections, and record, update and submit the cashbooks, RCD/RCR to COA, if issued; to present the missing accountable forms to COA, if unissued; or to give notices of loss in accordance of COA Circular no. 84-233 dated August 21, 1984.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive to recommend the budget officer to attend training that involved budgeting.
The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the required registries of funds / appropriations / allotments and obligations and utilization in order to avoid juggling of funds and for internal control purposes. Also, to facilitate the realization of the programs / projects / activities embodied in the Approved Annual Budget.
Submit the quarterly report statement of Allotment, Obligations and Balances and monthly the registries.
Strict adherence to the provision of laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant the following:
To suspend or withhold from the salaries of officials and employees the amount corresponding to their unsettled cash advances.
To follow up from the grantees the submission of their liquidation documents.
To stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those with unliquidated travel expenses vouchers.
Otherwise, appropriate disciplinary measures shall be imposed against them should they continue to violate Section 89 of PD 1445.

We have recommended to the LCE to direct the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports, post the same to appropriate website, bulletin board or conspicuous places, and to submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to COA. Submit proof that the bayanihan disbursements and procurements were posted in the GPPB online portal.

We have recommended the following:

Cause the repair of all defects noted and submit a complete report on the same to the Audit Team.

Make sure that all water supply system projects comply with the requirement on quality test for water, pursuant to DPWH Department Order No. 102 dated May 27, 2016
Planning Section conducts survey and investigations in accordance with the criteria and standards adopted by the agency to carry out accurate design and effective production of plans. They should first consider series of meeting in the community in order to ensure immediate implementation of the project when the funds are downloaded.
Municipal Engineer to constantly monitor the projects implemented if the projects were effectively used.
Submit the quarterly status of the projects implemented.

We have recommended to the Municipal Engineer / Municipal General Service Officer / Municipal Agricultural Officer to submit the above-mentioned documents for evaluation. And informed the source agency of the demolition and requested the demolished projects will be shouldered and transferred by the DPWH.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
In view of the computerization of the Commission On Audit in monitoring audit suspensions, disallowances and charges, to assign specific personnel to do the compliance of supporting documents and monitoring of it. Otherwise, after the ninety (90) day period of non-compliance, the system will automatically lapsed, notice of disallowance will immediately be required to the audit team for issuance.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to pre-audit all claims or disbursement of funds and require complete supporting documents before finally paying the claims and affix her signature to the Disbursement Vouchers before submission to the audit team to minimize audit suspensions.

b. The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted on the accounts property, plant and equipment based on the result of the actual inventory taking and reconcile with the accounting records. Also, to review the computed Accumulated Depreciation to determined its proper computation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive should ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash fund transfers in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2007-001. 
b. That the projects and programs to be implemented should be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines of the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) to avoid misuse and misapplication of funds.
c. The focal person of the BUB Projects should submit the SB Resolution, to determine the accreditation of the organization to the LGU and other supporting documents as required in the accreditation to be attached as part of the liquidation report.
d. Supposedly, after accreditation and proper evaluation, the project of the NGOs / POs be awarded to those who meet the minimum qualification requirements and which can satisfactorily undertake the project at terms most advantageous to the beneficiaries.
e. Supposedly, the Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness and validity of documents before releasing the cash advance to the NGOs/POs. Proper recording of the transaction in the books of accounts by recording the initial release to the asset account and recognizing expenses only upon submission of the liquidation report.
f. The Municipal Accountant should require from the NGO/POs concerned the submission of liquidation reports/accomplishment reports within reasonable time on the livelihood projects.
g. The focal person of the BUB projects is responsible to monitor and inspect the project implementation and verify financial records and reports of the NGOs/ POs.
h. The Municipal Accountant should avoid extending cash advances to those organizations who failed to liquidate the cash advances granted to them.
i. For delicadeza, the Municipal employees should refrain being the payees or beneficiaries of the NGO/POs otherwise there is a conflict of interest under the Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office indicated that it coordinated closely in order to determine the status of those NGOs/POs with pending unliquidated cash advances and their respective terminal reports in order to effect prompt liquidation. 

The Sangguniang Bayan Members commented during the initial exit conference on February 27, 2018 that these people’s organization were given recognition and accreditation with the promise that they will submit later their registration and other documentary requirements. The management acknowledged the deficiencies noted.

But the BUB focal person in the person of Mr. Fuentes asserted that registration in the DOLE, SEC, or DTI or even accreditation from the Sangguniang Bayan because the association were identified by the LPRAT.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive should properly maintain the government structure and if possible request immediate inspection from the Department of Agriculture for proper turnover to the beneficiaries.
b.  The Local Chief should explain in writing why until to date, the said projects were not turned over properly to the intended beneficiaries considering the lapse of time and the projects seemed to deteriorate and depreciate.
c.   The focal person of the Department of Agriculture should explain in writing why inspection was not immediately conducted after the completion of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Turnover was pending due to cancellation of travel of the Department of Agriculture Officials to inspect the project site according to the Management, however, a letter from the Regional Director of the Department of Agriculture provided that the above listed projects were already completed and turnover on the above various dates.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. Without prejudice in the filing of charges to those concerned personnel, the focal person of the PAMANA projects should explain in writing why the projects were constructed on a privately-owned lot. Otherwise, the transaction is illegal and may be disallowed in audit.
b. The Local Chief Executive should convince the owner of the lot to issue a deed of donation considering that the projects were already constructed.
b.   The Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the possible expenditures in the transfer of title.

We have recommended the following:
a.  The Planning / Engineering department to conduct survey and investigation in accordance with the criteria and standards adopted by the agency to carry out accurate design and effective production of plans. They should first consider series of meeting in the community in order to ensure immediate implementation of the project when the funds are downloaded.
b. The program of work should be prepared, reviewed and evaluated thoroughly to ensure that project design and estimates are realistic and cover all phases of the projects to minimize variation orders and time extensions which often entail increase in project cost and cause delay in the project completion.
c. For future guidance, the project should be bided as a whole project and awarded to the contractor. In case of deficiencies noted in the inspection, the management can impose liquidated damages to defaulting contractors; terminate / rescind contracts of infrastructure projects with negative slippages of more than ten (10) percent resulting from contractor’s fault or negligence. The project engineer should explain in writing why the implementations of those projects were by administration.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to comment on the delay of the project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive should properly maintain the government equipments and if possible maximize its uses.
b. The Project Monitoring Team should monitor whether the equipments were used by the intended beneficiaries.
c. The Local Government Unit services does not stop until turnover of equipment but rather monitor whether the purpose of the said projects was served and accomplished.
d. The beneficiary, Mr. Nasser Manalao should produce the missing equipments such as:
• one rice thresher;
• one power tiller with engine no. JG80DI-15080380;
• one corn sheller; and
• one rice cutter / harvester.
e. Failure to produce the missing items will warrant the filing of appropriate charges to the accountable personnel on the loss of the assets.
observations.managementAction: The Management in the person of Mr. Nasser D. Manalao commented that the equipments are to be used by any members of the MNLF since the project was given by the Office of the Presidential Adviser on the Peace Process (OPAPP) to the MNLF and not exclusively only to the Barangay of Delabayan, Kauswagan, Lanao del Norte.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Focal Person should conduct orientation on the guidelines and the submission of the liquidation reports and ensure the projects were received by the intended beneficiaries. 
b. The Municipal Accountant should require from the association concerned the submission of liquidation report / accomplishment report more specific the list of beneficiaries with their signatures signifying their acceptance/ acknowledgement of the project/funds/goods/services received within reasonable time on the livelihood projects even though those items were received by the association.
c. Subject the identified transaction for fraud audit due to the deficiencies noted and recommended for filing of appropriate charges against erring officials and accountable personnel.
observations.managementAction: Mr. Nasser D. Manalao acknowledged the observation noted and commented that they were not informed that they will submit listing of the beneficiaries who received the items. But, he revealed that the Municipality had already prepared the documents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a.  The Local Chief Executive should consider the skills in the installation of a water system and not just to be undertaken by Administration without special skills.
b.  Proper planning, survey of the source of water should be undertaken before finally implementing the projects otherwise funds will be wasted,
c.  The Municipal Planning Development Coordinator or Municipal Engineer should explain in writing why water supply will operate only for two hours a day.
d.  Refrain from implementing projects without first approving the amended program of work by the funding source.
observations.managementAction:
Engr. Eddie Tabanao in his affidavit paragraph no. 4 averred that when the 80% of the total cost of the project was released, the same was paid to the winning bidder. For although, it is the Local Government of Kauswagan that is the implementing agency, the materials used for the project underwent a bidding. The winning bidder got almost all the 80% fund for the materials and left only around P27,000.00 for payment of labor. Such became the problem because of the low compensation budget for labor and caused the delay in the implementation of the project.

Paragraph No. 9 in his affidavit averred also that with respect to the chlorine tank, initially, there was resistance from the community as to its construction. According to them, chlorine is harmful to mankind and besides, there has never been epidemic in their area. This added to the delay of the project. However, after intensive discussion and repeated caucus with the community, the latter was persuaded and allowed the installation of the chlorine tank. Picture shown below is the installation of the chlorination cage.

It was explained by Sultan Camlon T. Moner former ABC President of the Municipality of Kauswagan, that they did not allow putting chlorine in their water because it was already clean and safe for drinking. They do not want to put chlorine as they see it as a poison for them. That the construction is based on the actual situation or condition because the installation is based on the number of recipients / beneficiaries which are not considered in the preparation of the original program of work.

b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements monthly for all the funds of the Municipality and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income and staled checks and any adjustments monthly to bring back the cash in bank account in its correct balance.
b.	The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer as signatories of the bank accounts of the Municipality to authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll on i-access / online access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.
c.Request from the Local Chief Executive additional personnel in the Accounting Office to augment the workforce to facilitate the timely recording and preparation of financial reports.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

We have recommended the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly control cash advances and implement strictly Section 89 of P.D. 1445 specifically the policy that “No additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Direct further the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the guidelines stated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 specifically the following:
Only permanently appointed officials shall be designated as disbursing officers. Elected officials may be granted a cash advance only for their official travelling expenses.
Transfer of cash advances from one Accountable Officer to another shall not be allowed.
The Municipal Accountant should prepare an aging of unliquidated cash advances to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 as follows:
Absence of records or documents to validate / support the claim and / or unreconciled reciprocal accounts.
Death of the accountable officer / employee / debtor.
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer / employee / debtor, and that he / she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him / her.
Incapacity to pay or insolvency.
Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers.
No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.
Protect the interest of the government by requiring refund or implement the withholding of salaries and allowances of delinquent employees with unliquidated cash advances.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We have recommended to the accountable officer to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover her maximum cash accountability. Management should determine the maximum cash accountability to be assigned to the accountable officer before the application with the Bureau of Treasury. Require the Municipal Mayor to issue designation order for collecting officer and to allot in the annual budget additional fidelity bond for the municipal collectors.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer should report immediately the missing accountable forms inorder that a notice of loss will be circulated throughout.
The Municipal Treasurer should exert efforts to locate the missing accountable forms and submit a written explanation for such occurrence
The Municipal Treasurer shall verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuance to collectors and ending balances on hand.
Make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collectors and check the same against new balances on hand column.
A permanent and adequate record book containing all information shall be kept for control purposes as set forth in Sec. 95 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the AO to issue official receipts in strict numerical sequence to account the issuances of accountable forms in sequence and facilitate reconciliation.
We have also recommended to the accountant to review carefully the collections remitted by the Cashier to prevent the possible commission of irregularities thereby protecting the interest of the agency.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact all her collections to the depository bank of the Municipality.
Discontinue the practice of using collections to pay various expenses even without an existing appropriation.
Stop the practice of requesting the Sangguniang Bayan to cover an appropriation ordinance of expenses paid long time ago without an appropriation using the current appropriation because budget were approved annually.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a Monthly Reconciliation Statements with Bank Statements and other supporting documents from January 2013 to July 2016 that were not submitted to COA for verification by the resident auditor. Prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis together with their supporting documents to the Resident Auditor concerned within 10 days after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly control cash advances and implement strictly Section 89 of P.D. 1445 specifically the policy that “No additional cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
Direct further the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the guidelines stated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 specifically the following:
Only permanently appointed officials shall be designated as disbursing officers. Elected officials may be granted a cash advance only for their official travelling expenses.
Transfer of cash advances from one Accountable Officer to another shall not be allowed.
The Municipal Accountant should prepare an aging of unliquidated cash advances to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 as follows:
Absence of records or documents to validate / support the claim and / or unreconciled reciprocal accounts.
Death of the accountable officer / employee / debtor.
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer / employee / debtor, and that he / she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him / her.
Incapacity to pay or insolvency.
Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers.
No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.
Protect the interest of the government by requiring refund or implement the withholding of salaries and allowances of delinquent employees with unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendation:

The General Services Office should adopt the proper control and regulation in the use of government vehicles. Submit monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of all government motor vehicles to the Audit Team within the first ten (10) days of the succeeding month. See to it that only authorized vehicles and heavy equipment bearing red plate number to refill fuel to accredited fuel station.
The Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer who certifies to the existence of appropriation, the completeness of supporting documents and the availability of funds should carefully examine all the supporting documents before payment will be effected to avoid future accountability in the performance of official function.

Recommendation:

This will guarantee that all properties are adequately covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. Likewise, request for budget on the premiums to be paid to GSIS as soon as computation will be received from GSIS.
